title: Beyond Recall@k: A Multi-Dimensional Evaluation Framework for Retrieval-Augmented Generation
abstract: Existing evaluation of retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) systems typically reduces to Recall@k of retrieved passages or end-task accuracy, neither of which captures the full quality of the retrieval-generation interaction. We propose RAGEval, an evaluation framework that separately scores retrieval relevance, citation faithfulness, answer correctness, and answer-context alignment. We release a benchmark of 5,200 questions spanning open-domain QA, multi-hop reasoning, and long-document QA. Using RAGEval we show that high Recall@k can mask citation hallucination in 14-22% of generated answers, and that current RAG systems trade citation faithfulness for fluency. Our framework is independent of retrieval method (dense, sparse, hybrid) and generator architecture.
